chunks.text: e/b/pub/ssuprem3.help July 1, 2004 *********************************************************************** Mingus.eecs has been retired from service (July 1, 2004) and this software is no longer available. See

d this software is no longer available. See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/clients.cgi?choice=13 for a list of other Instructional login servers. See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/software.html for ref

inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/software.html for reference to software that is available on the other servers. *********************************************************************** Silvaco SSUMREM3 on EECS Instructional Systems ~~~~~~~~~~~~~

REM3 on EECS Instructional Systems ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ CONTENTS: Silvaco SSUPREM3 SSUPREM3 Licenses Silvaco VWF Tools Other CAD Tools Silvaco SSUPREM3 ---------------- The SSUPREM3 program from Silvaco Internati

---- The SSUPREM3 program from Silvaco International is available for EECS Instructional users on the UNIX system called mingus.eecs.berkeley.edu. To use SSUPREM3, you must have X Windows running on your local workstation. All UNIX workstati

ng on your local workstation. All UNIX workstations have X Windows. All EECS Instructional WinNT/2K systems have the an X Windows manager called Exceed. UC Berkeley students can get Exceed for home use for free. See http://inst.eecs.berk

r home use for free. See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html Here are the steps for running SSUPREM3: 1) Start XWindow manager if necessary (Exceed, etc) 2) Login to mingus.eecs.berkeley.edu (using 'ssh') 3) 'cd' to the

ecs.berkeley.edu (using 'ssh') 3) 'cd' to the directory where you want to store files 4) Set your UNIX path with these commands: set SILVACO = /share/instsww/silvaco set path = ( $SILVACO/hpux11/bin $SILVACO/eecad/bin $path);

( $SILVACO/hpux11/bin $SILVACO/eecad/bin $path); rehash 5) Check out a license: (EECS students only; SEE "Licenses" BELOW) Run: /share/instsww/silvaco/eecad/bin/eecad_access An "e*ECAD Remote Access" window will pop up.

"e*ECAD Remote Access" window will pop up. Enter Login: eecsinst Enter Password: [ ask your instructor to ask inst@eecs for access ] You only need to check out a license once, each time you login. 6) Run: /share/instsww/s

each time you login. 6) Run: /share/instsww/silvaco/hpux11/bin/deckbuild & To run a demonstration: 6) Right-click on "Main Control", select "Examples..." 7) Scroll to bottom, Double-left-click on "SSUPREM3: 1-D Process Simula

Double-left-click on "SSUPREM3: 1-D Process Simulation" Double-Left-click on "MOS1: MOS Application Examples" or etc Double-Left-click on an input file, such as "s3ex01.in" Read about the example. 8) Click on "Load Example".

bout the example. 8) Click on "Load Example". It will appear in the initial deckbuild window. 9) In the deckbuild window, click "run". Additional information in in Adobe Acroread format, which you can read on Mingus using the UNI

mat, which you can read on Mingus using the UNIX commands acroread /share/instsww/silvaco/docs/ssuprem3_um.pdf & acroread /share/instsww/silvaco/docs/vwf-v1.pdf & SSUPREM3 Licenses ----------------- SSUPREM3 is being funded by pay-per-use l

------ SSUPREM3 is being funded by pay-per-use licensing via http://www.eecad.com. EECS Instruction has established a line of credit there that is only for use by students in EECS classes. EECS students can download SSUPREM3 onto their own

ECS students can download SSUPREM3 onto their own computers from http://www.eecad.com. They are allowed to use "eecsinst" account to fund their use of SSUPREM3 as long as they are using it for EECS coursework. Others users can establish thei

ECS coursework. Others users can establish their own lines of credit by creating an account and providing a credit card number; see http://www.eecad.com for details. Silvaco VWF Tools ----------------- Our licenses for the Silvaco Virtual Wa

-------- Our licenses for the Silvaco Virtual Wafer Fab (VWF) tools expired in April 1999. We did not renew this software because it was no longer being used. The latest version had been installed on parker.eecs in November 1995. Other

talled on parker.eecs in November 1995. Other CAD Tools --------------- For references to other CAD tools that are available on EECS Instructional computers, please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~cad http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/s

erkeley.edu/~cad http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/software http://www.silvaco.com and /share/b/pub/cad.help Stanford University still provides suprem3 from http://www-tcad.stanford.edu/, but it is unsupported. EECS Instructional Suppor
t is unsupported. EECS Instructional Support Group 378/384/386 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
